title: pelargonium × atrorubens
intro: Pelargonium × atrorubens, a hybrid plant species, is a cross between Pelargonium atrorubens and Pelargonium fasciculatum, commonly known as a type of geranium. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × atrorubens include relief from cold and flu symptoms, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
